subject: GeneralKnowledge
title: GeneralKnowledge Test - 28
description: A mix of fun and educational questions covering animals, geography, science, community, and health for Grade 4 students.

questions.q: Which animal is famous for being the 'King of the Jungle'?
questions.explanation: Lions are widely known as the 'King of the Jungle' due to their strength and the impressive mane on the males.
questions.hint: It is a member of the cat family.
questions.objective: Identify common animals and their nicknames.

questions.q: What is the capital of Italy?
questions.explanation: Rome is the capital of Italy and is known for its incredible history and ancient ruins.
questions.hint: It is home to the Colosseum.

questions.q: Which part of the plant absorbs water and minerals from the soil?
questions.explanation: Roots are responsible for anchoring the plant and pulling water and nutrients up from the ground.

questions.q: What do we call the imaginary line that divides Earth into the Northern and Southern Hemispheres?
questions.explanation: The equator is an imaginary circle around the Earth that divides it into two halves.

questions.q: Which of these is a healthy snack for your body?
questions.explanation: Apples are a fruit filled with vitamins and fiber, making them a much healthier choice than processed snacks.
questions.hint: It is crunchy and grows on trees.

questions.q: Which community helper works in a library?
questions.explanation: Librarians manage the library and help people find books, resources, and information.

questions.q: What is the name of the nearest star to Earth?
questions.explanation: The Sun is the closest star to Earth and provides the light and energy needed for life.
questions.hint: We see it during the day.

questions.q: Which of these is a non-renewable energy source?
questions.explanation: Coal is a fossil fuel that takes millions of years to form and cannot be replaced once it is used up.

questions.q: What is the boiling point of water in degrees Celsius?
questions.explanation: Water boils at 100 degrees Celsius at sea level.

questions.q: Which season comes right after winter?
questions.explanation: Spring follows winter, bringing warmer weather and the blooming of flowers.
